i-seed00001.description:
Platform v2.0 Migration

Migrate the entire platform to v2.0 architecture. This is a large initiative covering authentication, database, and API changes.

## Goals
- Modernize authentication with OAuth2
- Migrate database schema for new entity model
- Implement API rate limiting
- Update deployment pipeline

## Timeline
Targeting completion by end of Q2.
i-seed00001.creator: alice
i-seed00001.progress: Phase 1 (auth) is underway. Database migration planning complete.

i-seed00002.creator: alice
i-seed00002.progress: Provider selected (Keycloak). Authorization code flow implemented. Token refresh in progress.

i-seed00003.description:
Implement OAuth2 token refresh logic

Add automatic token refresh when access tokens expire. Must handle concurrent requests gracefully and queue refresh operations.
i-seed00003.creator: bob

i-seed00004.description:
Add OAuth2 scopes and permissions support

Implement fine-grained authorization using OAuth2 scopes. Map existing role-based permissions to scope definitions.
i-seed00004.creator: alice

todo_list.description: Update API docs with scope requirements

i-seed00005.description:
Database schema migration for v2.0

Migrate the PostgreSQL schema to support new entity relationships and the audit log table.
i-seed00005.creator: alice
i-seed00005.progress: Migration scripts written and tested. Deployed to staging successfully.

i-seed00006.description:
Implement API rate limiting

Add rate limiting middleware to protect API endpoints from abuse. Use a sliding window algorithm with Redis backing.
i-seed00006.creator: alice
i-seed00006.progress: Initial implementation had memory leak in Redis connection pool. Investigation ongoing.

i-seed00007.description:
Fix login page 500 error on expired sessions

Users are seeing a 500 error when their session expires and they try to access the login page. The session cleanup middleware is throwing an unhandled exception.
i-seed00007.creator: dev-user
i-seed00007.progress: Root cause identified: session middleware not handling null session gracefully. Fix deployed.

i-seed00008.description:
Add dark mode support

Implement a dark mode theme that users can toggle from their profile settings. Should respect the OS-level preference by default.

## Requirements
- Toggle in user profile settings
- Respect `prefers-color-scheme` media query
- Persist preference in localStorage
- Smooth transition animation between themes
- All components must support both themes
i-seed00008.creator: dev-user

todo_list.description: Create theme token system (colors, shadows, borders)
Update all component styles for dark theme

i-seed00009.description:
Improve search performance

The full-text search endpoint is too slow for large datasets. Need to add proper indexing and consider Elasticsearch integration.
i-seed00009.creator: alice
i-seed00009.progress: Profiling complete. Main bottleneck is the unindexed LIKE query on description field. Adding GIN index.

todo_list.description: Add GIN index on description column

i-seed00010.description:
Update deployment documentation

The deployment docs are outdated after the Kubernetes migration. Need to update with new kubectl commands and Helm chart references.
i-seed00010.creator: bob

i-seed00011.description:
Review: OAuth2 provider integration patch

Please review the OAuth2 provider integration patch (p-seed00001). Focus areas:
- Security of token handling
- Error handling edge cases
- Test coverage
i-seed00011.creator: bob

i-seed00012.description:
Merge: Rate limiter middleware

Merge the rate limiter middleware patch after addressing review feedback.
i-seed00012.creator: alice

p-seed00001.title: Add OAuth2 provider integration
p-seed00001.description:
Integrates Keycloak as the OAuth2 / OIDC provider. Implements authorization code flow with PKCE, token validation middleware, and user info endpoint.

Key changes:
- New `auth/oauth2.ts` module with provider configuration
- Updated `middleware/auth.ts` to validate JWT tokens
- Added `routes/auth/callback.ts` for OAuth2 callback handling
- New environment variables: `OAUTH2_ISSUER_URL`, `OAUTH2_CLIENT_ID`
p-seed00001.creator: bob

reviews.contents:
Looks good overall. A few questions:
1. Should we add token caching to reduce calls to the OIDC provider?
2. The error handling in the callback route could be more specific.
3. Consider adding a health check endpoint that verifies OIDC provider connectivity.
reviews.author: alice

p-seed00002.title: Implement OAuth2 scopes and permission mapping
p-seed00002.description: Maps existing RBAC permissions to OAuth2 scopes. Adds scope validation middleware and updates all protected endpoints.
p-seed00002.creator: alice

reviews.contents: LGTM! Clean implementation. The scope hierarchy is well thought out.
reviews.author: bob

p-seed00003.title: Add rate limiter middleware
p-seed00003.description: Implements a sliding window rate limiter using Redis. Configurable per-endpoint limits with different tiers for authenticated and anonymous users.
p-seed00003.creator: bob

reviews.contents: The sliding window implementation has a race condition between INCR and EXPIRE. Use a Lua script or MULTI/EXEC to make it atomic. Also, the Redis connection pool isn't being closed on shutdown which causes the memory leak reported in i-seed00006.
reviews.author: alice

failure.name: integration-tests
failure.summary: Rate limiter integration tests timed out waiting for Redis connection

p-seed00004.title: Update API documentation for v2.0
p-seed00004.description: Comprehensive update to the API documentation reflecting all v2.0 changes including new OAuth2 endpoints, updated request/response schemas, and rate limiting headers.

p-seed00004.creator: dev-user

d-seed00001.title: ADR-001: OAuth2 Migration Strategy
d-seed00001.body_markdown:
# ADR-001: OAuth2 Migration Strategy

## Status
Accepted

## Context
Our current session-based authentication does not scale well across multiple services and lacks standardized token exchange capabilities needed for our microservices migration.

## Decision
We will migrate to OAuth2 / OpenID Connect using Keycloak as the identity provider.

### Key decisions:
1. **Provider**: Keycloak (self-hosted) for maximum control
2. **Flow**: Authorization Code with PKCE for web apps
3. **Token format**: JWT with RS256 signing
4. **Session migration**: Gradual rollout with feature flags

## Consequences
- Users will need to re-authenticate once during migration
- All services must validate JWT tokens independently
- Adds operational complexity of managing Keycloak

d-seed00002.title: API Reference Guide v2.0
d-seed00002.body_markdown:
# API Reference Guide v2.0

## Base URL
`https://api.acme.dev/v2`

## Authentication
All requests require a Bearer token:
```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
```

## Endpoints

### Issues
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | /v1/issues | List all issues |
| POST | /v1/issues | Create a new issue |
| GET | /v1/issues/:id | Get issue details |
| PUT | /v1/issues/:id | Update an issue |
| DELETE | /v1/issues/:id | Delete an issue |

### Jobs
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | /v1/jobs | List all jobs |
| POST | /v1/jobs | Create a new job |
| GET | /v1/jobs/:id | Get job details |

## Rate Limiting
All endpoints are rate-limited. Check the `X-RateLimit-Remaining` header.

d-seed00003.title: Developer Onboarding Guide
d-seed00003.body_markdown:
# Developer Onboarding Guide

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites
- Node.js 20+
- Docker and Docker Compose
- Access to the GitHub organization

### Setup
1. Clone the repository: `git clone https://github.com/acme/web-app.git`
2. Install dependencies: `pnpm install`
3. Copy config: `cp .env.example .env`
4. Start services: `docker compose up -d`
5. Run migrations: `pnpm migrate`
6. Start dev server: `pnpm dev`

### Project Structure
```
src/
  auth/       - Authentication modules
  middleware/ - Express middleware
  routes/     - API route handlers
  models/     - Database models
  utils/      - Shared utilities
```

## Common Tasks
- **Run tests**: `pnpm test`
- **Lint**: `pnpm lint`
- **Build**: `pnpm build`
